delivered.fact: Octopuses actively edit over 60% of the RNA in their nervous system in response to water temperature changes. This doesn't alter their permanent DNA — instead it rewires brain protein function on the fly to adapt to cold conditions within hours. This mechanism was confirmed in 2023 studies from the Marine Biological Laboratory, published in Cell and verified independently by Smithsonian and Scientific American.

Scientists have found native bacteria and fungi living on melting alpine glaciers that can digest plastic at 15°C, much colder than previously known plastic-eating microbes. These organisms were isolated from the 'plastisphere' of Arctic and Swiss Alpine soils, breaking down biodegradable plastics without requiring the 30°C+ heat most similar species need. This discovery came from Swiss Federal Institute research verified by independent confirmations in 2023, 2024 and 2025 studies.
